creator: george@m5p.com
text:
Sorry, I should have said I'm running 13.1-RELEASE-p7.  And my options are:
OPTIONS_FILE_UNSET+=CANBERRA
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=DBUS
OPTIONS_FILE_UNSET+=DEBUG
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=FFMPEG
OPTIONS_FILE_UNSET+=LIBPROXY
OPTIONS_FILE_UNSET+=LTO
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=OPTIMIZED_CFLAGS
OPTIONS_FILE_UNSET+=PROFILE
OPTIONS_FILE_UNSET+=TEST
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=ALSA
OPTIONS_FILE_UNSET+=JACK
OPTIONS_FILE_UNSET+=PULSEAUDIO
OPTIONS_FILE_UNSET+=SNDIO
I'm not sure what you are asking, regarding audio.
I don't think this has anything to do with the display driver in use; it simply puts up a text message on the screen saying that the file is corrupt, without (as far as I can tell) even trying to display it.
